ground_truth.title: Different Types of Cameras for Photography and Video Explained
ground_truth.author: Alyssa Maio
ground_truth.main_content: There are a million different types of cameras on the market. Which one should you choose? Well aside from reading endless specs, one way to figure it out is to simply know the categories of cameras available to you. Do you want a mirrorless or a quick point and shoot? Is a DSLR better for your occasional impromptu video shoots? Knowing the different types of cameras will help you determine which is best for your projects. The article covers seven main types: Compact Cameras including standard point-and-shoot, zoom compacts, and advanced compacts; DSLR Cameras known for their optical viewfinder and professional-level quality; Mirrorless Cameras as a lighter, more compact alternative; Action or Adventure Cameras like GoPros for outdoor and sports; 360 Cameras for immersive footage; Medium Format Cameras with their large sensors and exceptional image quality; and Traditional Film Cameras for that nostalgic look. Each section includes pros, cons, and specific product recommendations.
with: There are a million different types of cameras on the market. Which one should you choose?

DSLRs are the go-to cameras for most creatives. Whether you're a professional photographer or aspiring videographer looking to get their YouTube channel off the ground, you'll likely find this camera works for any project.
Mirrorless cameras are a lighter, more compact alternative to the DSLR, and provide incredible quality images, especially video.
Action cameras, sometimes called Adventure cameras, are great for outdoor filming, or otherwise rough conditions. They're often used for sports photography and videography.
Medium format takes pictures that are just smaller than a large format size but bigger than using 135mm or full-frame sensors.
without: Share your vision with elegant shot lists and storyboards
